## Recent Events

*(chronological log of NFGC-specific observations)*

- [2026-04-25] New Found Gold Corp (NFGC) has successfully navigated the 'Orphan Period' of the Lassonde Curve by securing a $205M financing package, transitioning it from a speculative explorer to a funded developer with a H2 2027 production target.

- [2026-04-26] Management may 'sandbag' or tolerate high AISC on satellite deposits (e.g., Hammerdown) to use them as cash-flow-neutral bridges to maintain permits and operational infrastructure for higher-grade core assets.

- [2026-04-26] High AISC on secondary production assets (like NFG's Hammerdown) may be strategically acceptable if the project functions to fund corporate overhead and secure mill access for higher-margin flagship ore.

- [2026-04-26] Large-scale institutional lead orders (e.g., EdgePoint's $205M package) during the developer transition phase serve as a psychological 'floor' for retail sentiment and effectively neutralize dilution-related bear cases.

- [2026-04-27] For New Found Gold (NFG), the retail community views insider option exercises by exploration leadership as a more significant high-conviction signal than standard market buying.

- [2026-04-27] New Found Gold Corp (NFGC) has shifted from an exploration-only story to a developer lifecycle stage following the acquisition of Maritime Resources and the Pine Cove Mill, targeting first gold in H2 2026.

- [2026-04-27] New Found Gold (NFG) is attempting to bypass the mining 'Orphan Period' by acquiring Maritime Resources (MAE), gaining the Pine Cove Mill (1,300 t/d capacity) and the Hammerdown mine to reach production by H2 2025/2026.

- [2026-04-27] High-grade orogenic gold deposits modeled after Fosterville (like Queensway) often carry a retail expectation that actual mined grades will significantly exceed initial drill grades.

- [2026-04-27] NFGC retail conviction is heavily anchored to specific 'North Star' investors (like Eric Sprott), where tracking the primary investor's family/affiliate holdings provides a psychological floor for the community.

- [2026-04-27] Retail expectations for NFGC are anchored to a post-producer 'fair value' of $6.00–$8.00 CAD, with long-term targets reaching $12–$25 CAD.

- [2026-04-27] NFGC's strategic pivot to a 'Hub and Spoke' model via the Pine Cove Mill acquisition shifts its retail valuation driver from drill results (gram-meters) to H2 2026/2027 production timelines.

- [2026-04-27] The 'Sprott Shield'—a term for high insider ownership (32% in NFGC's case)—serves as a psychological price floor for retail investors, mitigating capitulation during steep price corrections.

- [2026-04-27] NFGC's orogenic system (Fosterville-style) requires aggressive top-cutting of grades to reconcile 'Bonanza Grade' retail expectations with realistic bulk-tonnage economics for starter mines.
